title: Plan-Control Compiler FAIL: compilation_claims.move_sequence inconsistent with both referenced certs (GENERATOR_SEQUENCE_MISMATCH)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates GENERATOR_SEQUENCE_MISMATCH: the compiler cert correctly finds and hash-verifies both referenced certs (planner and control both have moves [increase_amplitude, set_frequency]). However compilation_claims.move_sequence declares ['increase_amplitude', 'increase_frequency'] — the second move is wrong (increase_frequency instead of set_frequency). Checks CC1-CC7 and CC9 pass; only CC8 fails. The two referenced certs are internally consistent with each other; the failure is in the compiler cert's own claim about the compilation. This proves the validator catches stale or incorrect move-sequence claims even when both referenced certs are valid and agree.

validation_checks.description: source planner cert found in domain fixtures
source planner cert hash matches declared hash
target control cert found in domain fixtures
target control cert hash matches declared hash

fail_ledger.message: compilation_claims.move_sequence=['increase_amplitude','increase_frequency'] does not match the extracted move sequences from both referenced certs: planner=[increase_amplitude,set_frequency] and control=[increase_amplitude,set_frequency]. The second move 'increase_frequency' in the claims does not appear in either cert's actual generator sequence. Both referenced certs are consistent with each other — the mismatch is in the compiler cert's own claim. Likely cause: stale or incorrect compilation_claims.move_sequence authored against a different version of the control cert.
